---
title: "D4 EV Transition Economics"
format: revealjs
---

## The Question

Should a consumer switch to an EV?

We answer with two simple outputs:

- Is the switch practical for the commute and local charging context?
- What is the expected annual savings, `<S_annual>`?

## What Drives the Answer

Fuel prices, electricity rates, commute length, incentives, charging density, and the EV price premium matter more than broad EV averages.

The same vehicle can be a strong financial choice in one state and a marginal choice in another.

## Modeling Approach

We built separate classification and regression workflows.

The ladder keeps a baseline visible, then compares linear models, decision trees, and tuned XGBoost.

All preprocessing is fitted inside the training split to prevent leakage.

## Recommendation

Use the app as a buying assistant, not a guarantee.

Buyers with high commute mileage, high gas prices, moderate utility rates, incentives, and charging access tend to see the best case.

When infrastructure density is low, verify charging before relying on the savings estimate.
